reasoning: Iteration 1 improved Q1 to 55.47ms, now 1.55x slower than DuckDB (86ms). The 36% performance gap is significant with concrete, high-confidence optimizations available: SIMD filtering (8-15% gain), loop unrolling (5-10%), and lock-free aggregation (2-5%). These are low-risk CPU optimizations targeting the identified bottleneck.
focus_areas: Activate unused SIMD vectorized date filtering (simd_filter_dates) for 8-date/cycle throughput
Increase loop unroll factor from 4 to 8-16 for better instruction-level parallelism
Eliminate mutex lock overhead via thread-local aggregation with single-threaded merge
strategy_notes: Prioritize the simd_filter_dates() activation (rec. 0) as the highest-impact change — this is a complete optimization with existing infrastructure. Combine with loop unrolling (rec. 1) to expose more parallelism to the out-of-order engine. Use thread-local partitioning (rec. 2) to reduce synchronization overhead on 32 threads. These three changes target the CPU-bound bottleneck directly and have minimal interaction risk. Expected cumulative gain: 15-25%, bringing Q1 close to DuckDB performance.
notes: Avoid recommendations 4-5 (madvise prefetching, arithmetic fusion) — marginal gains on warm cache. Focus on the three high-impact CPU optimizations that directly address the scalar filtering and ILP bottlenecks.
